FyldCard
Configure virtual card programs for field events and internal employees. Powered by Stripe Issuing.
1
Program Status
FyldCard Program
Enable to activate both card programs
Enabled
Stripe Connection
Required to issue virtual cards
Connected · Verified
Funding Pools
Balance and top-up managed in Financial Management
Event Card Pool $4,820
Employee Card Pool $340 ⚠
View in Financial Management →
2
Event Card Rules
Rules, reclaim timing, and alert thresholds for event-type cards
Low balance alert threshold
Notify Financial Admin when Event Card Pool drops below this amount
$
Alert sent to Financial Admin email
Automatic load timing. FyldHub loads the card at worker check-in if Stripe Issuing responds instantly, or 30 minutes before scheduled event start time if there is any processing delay. No configuration required.
Reclaim grace period
Time after checkout selfie before unused funds are pulled back
Allows pending transactions to settle
Missed checkout auto-void
Card freezes automatically if worker does not check out
After scheduled event end time
Notify on reclaim
Email program manager when funds are pulled back
Receipts
Purchase documentation requirement
Receipts are collected automatically via the mobile task list — workers upload a receipt photo after each purchase as part of the event workflow. No additional configuration needed.
Load amount override
Who can approve overrides above the default load when posting an event
Requires approval from a Financial Admin — configured in User Roles
3
Spend Controls
Platform-managed · applies to all FyldCard programs
FyldHub restricts FyldCard spending to merchant categories relevant to field activation work. This prevents misuse without requiring brands to configure merchant codes manually.
Permitted Merchant Categories
Grocery / Liquor Retail Bar & Restaurant Supplies Beverage Wholesale Event & Party Supplies Rideshare (Uber / Lyft) Parking Office Supplies Printing & Signage
— reviewed by FyldHub within 2 business days
4
Event Card · Load Amounts
Toggle per event type · set default and maximum card load
Default load overridable at event creation · max load requires Financial Admin approval

5
Employee Card Program
Persistent cards for internal users · funded from the Employee Card Pool
Employee Card Program
Issue virtual or physical cards to internal team members
Disabled
Card type
Choose per user at card issuance
Card issuance & approval
Individual approval required for every card
Every card requires individual approval from a Financial Admin, regardless of the user's role. Spend limits and card type are set at the time of approval. Cards are managed in the Employee Cards section under Financial Management.
Spend limits
Set per user at card issuance
Monthly spend limits are configured individually when each card is approved. No account-level default — each cardholder's limit reflects their specific role and needs. Limits can be adjusted by a Financial Admin at any time without reissuing the card.
Receipt & expense reporting
How employee cardholders document their spending
Receipt upload window
after transaction
Concur
Expensify ✓
Ramp
Brex
QuickBooks
+ Other
Stripe transaction data will be forwarded to your selected expense system via API. Contact support to complete the integration setup.
Employees manage receipts and expense reports outside of FyldHub. Stripe transaction data is still available in Financial Management for reconciliation.
Changes apply immediately to new cards. Existing active cards are not affected.

Org Hierarchy
Name Job Title Role Brand Scope Location Scope Channel Hub Access Status Action
No users yet
Invite your team to get started. Each user is assigned a role and scope.
This tab shows your internal platform users (web app only). Workforce users — Brand Promoters, FyldCheckers, and FyldRunners — are managed separately via the mobile app. To manage outside agency and distribution partners, click the Outside Partners tab above.
Hub Access: BABrand Activations MEMerchandising PHASE 2 ODOn-Demand Support PHASE 2
Roles & Permissions
13 predefined roles. Click any role to view or edit its permissions. Create custom roles using any predefined role as a starting point.
System Roles
Account Owner System
Billing, subscription management, full user and role administration. One per account. Cannot be deleted — only transferred.
0 users
Programs, Workforce, Brand…
Administrator System
Full operational access to all platform modules. Can manage users, roles, and all settings. Cannot access billing.
0 users
Programs, Workforce, Brand…
Operational Roles
Program Owner
Creates and owns programs and templates. Final approval on launches and briefs. Approves budget draws within scope.
0 users
Programs, Workforce, Brand…
Program Manager
Manages active programs and events. Creates events under approved programs. Cannot create programs or manage budget pools.
0 users
Programs, Workforce, Brand…
Field Team
Field-facing execution. Submits activation requests, shelf tasks, and support requests. Can be assigned as Internal Event Staff.
0 users
Programs, Brand, Markets…
Workforce Coordinator
Human touchpoint for the worker side. Sends invites, reviews applications, accepts or declines. Point of contact until AI automation takes over.
0 users
Programs, Workforce, Brand…
Finance Manager
Creates and owns budget pools. Approves spend and payment requests. Full financial reporting. Cannot manage users or create events.
0 users
Programs, Brand, Markets…
Finance Contributor
Inputs expense data, uploads receipts, pulls financial reports. Cannot approve, create budget pools, or process payments.
0 users
Financials, Reporting
Ops Manager
Full asset and logistics management. Kit and POS inventory, shipment tracking, replenishment, FyldRunner task management.
0 users
Programs, Workforce, Brand…
Ops Contributor
Executes logistics and asset tasks. Processes replenishment requests, coordinates kit delivery.
0 users
Logistics, Reporting
Market Viewer
Territory and account visibility only. Views activation calendar and execution data for assigned markets. No ability to create or approve.
0 users
Programs, Brand, Markets…
Read-Only Roles
Analyst / Viewer
Full read-only access across reporting, analytics, and execution data within scope. Cannot create, edit, approve, or submit anything.
0 users
Programs, Workforce, Brand…
Compliance Viewer
Read-only access to audit trails, geo-stamped records, photo documentation, and compliance reports only.
0 users
Reporting, Audit
Scope always applies. Even with Full access on any module, a user only sees and acts on data within their assigned Brand, Territory, Location, and Channel scope.
Account Owner
System role
System role — permissions are locked. To create an editable version, click Copy as Custom Role.

Add New Role
1 Name & Base
2 Set Permissions
Your selection pre-fills all permissions on the next step. You can change anything.
Program Owner
Creates programs, approves launches
Program Manager
Manages active programs and events
Field Team
Field execution, requests, tasks
Workforce Coordinator
Worker invites and applications
Finance Manager
Budget pools and approvals
Ops Manager
Assets and logistics
Analyst / Viewer
Full read-only access
Blank
No Access on all modules
Permissions for this role
Starting point — you can change anything on the next step
Click any module to expand and configure permissions. Permissions are pre-filled from your chosen base role — adjust any feature.
Outside Partners
Agencies, distributors, retailers, and other external organizations with scoped access to your FyldHub account. All data created by outside partners belongs to your account.
Path 1 — Distributor / Retailer: Future FyldHub tenants — upgrade path in Phase 2/3
Path 2 — Custom Label: Agencies, brokers, 3PLs, consultants — no upgrade path
Delegated Execution: Partner actively executes work inside your account — data ownership stays with you
No outside partners yet
Add agencies, distributors, retailers, 3PLs, and other external organizations with scoped access to your account.
Data ownership is non-negotiable. Every event, program, photo, check-in record, and compliance document created by an outside partner belongs to your FyldHub account — not the agency. Partners never see each other's data, your internal financials, or data outside their assigned scope. Workers are always paid directly by you via FyldHub — never through an agency.
Org Hierarchy
Your org hierarchy controls team assignment reach and data roll-up visibility — independently of role permissions. Users higher in the tree see all data from users below them in their branch.
Using default 5-tier structure. Tier labels are customizable — click any label to rename. Switch to Custom Tree to build a Salesforce-style named hierarchy with unlimited depth and custom node names.
Tier Label Configuration
T1Can assign anyone
T2Assigns T3 and below
T3Assigns T4 and below
T4Assigns T5 only
T5No assignment
Roll-Up Visibility Rules
T1 ExecutiveSees all data in account
T2 DirectorSees T3–T5 in their branch
T3 ManagerSees T4–T5 in their branch
T4 LeadSees T5 directly below
T5 IndividualSees own data only
Scope still applies within tier reach. A T2 Director can assign T3 users — but only those sharing the same Brand and Territory scope.
No hierarchy configured yet
Add your first tier node to build out your org tree. The tier labels above define visibility roll-up rules.
Custom Tree (Salesforce-style): Click "Switch to Custom Tree" above to build a fully custom named hierarchy — unlimited depth, any node names, no tier count limit. The custom tree drives the same roll-up visibility and assignment-reach logic as the default tiers. Dev team determines MVP vs. Phase 2 based on complexity.
Custom Hierarchy Builder
Build your own named org tree. Add nodes, name them anything, connect parent-child relationships. Users placed on a node see all data from users on child nodes below them in their branch.
No custom nodes yet
Create your root node first (typically the Account Owner position), then add children beneath it. Each node gets a name, a color, and users assigned to it.

Connect FyldHub to your existing tools. Your field marketing data syncs with financial, CRM, and productivity systems so you don't re-enter data in multiple places.

ERP Systems
SAP
SAP S/4HANA
Budget sync, GL entries
Not Connected
ORC
Oracle NetSuite
Budget import, AP sync
Not Connected
CSV
CSV / Excel Import
Upload budget files directly
Available
CRM
SF
Salesforce
Account & contact sync
Not Connected
HS
HubSpot
Contact and deal sync
Not Connected
Productivity & BI
G
Google Workspace
Sheets, Calendar, Drive
Connected
M365
Microsoft 365
Excel, Outlook, SharePoint
Not Connected
TB
Tableau
Reporting & visualization
Not Connected
PBI
Power BI
Custom dashboards
Not Connected
Payments & HR / Payroll
SC
Stripe Connect
Worker payments (direct)
Connected
ADP
ADP
Payroll integration
Not Connected
GU
Gusto
HR & payroll sync
Not Connected
Logistics & Fulfillment
Carrier APIs are managed by FyldHub and activate automatically when the Logistics Add-on is enabled.
EP
EasyPost
Rate shopping & label gen
FyldHub Managed
SS
ShipStation
Order management & tracking
FyldHub Managed
UPS
UPS Locator
UPS Access Point finder
FyldHub Managed
FDX
FedEx
FedEx locator & direct ship
FyldHub Managed
Access & Permissions
View the permission matrix across all 9 roles and 11 modules. Edit permissions on the Roles tab, or configure status transition rules and data access controls below.
Permissions Overview
All 9 roles × 11 modules. Click any cell to edit that role's permissions.
Module Account Owner Admin Program Mgr Field Init. Staffing Finance Ops Coord. Analyst Compliance
Programs & EventsFullFullMgmtContribViewViewContribViewView
Workforce & StaffingFullFullMgmt—Full—ContribViewView
Brand & CatalogFullFullViewViewViewViewViewView—
Markets & LocationsFullFullViewViewContribViewViewView—
Financials & BudgetFullFullView——FullViewView—
Logistics & AssetsFullFullContribContribMgmtViewFullView—
Reporting & AnalyticsFullFullViewViewViewFullViewFullView
Payments & PayrollFullFull———Full—View—
Audit Trails & ComplianceFullFullViewViewViewViewViewViewFull
Exceptions & IncidentsFullFullMgmtContribContribViewMgmtViewView
Settings & User MgmtFullFull———————
Edit permissions per role on the Roles tab →
Status Transition Rules

Configure which roles can trigger each event status change. These override the module-level permissions for specific transitions.

TransitionDefault Minimum RoleYour Override
Draft → OpenProgram Manager
Post-Event → ApprovedProgram Manager
Any → Cancelled (pre-Live)Program Manager
Any → Cancelled (Live+)Administrator
Budget pool → ReallocateFinance Lead
Pay Rate Permissions

Control which roles can view, modify, and approve worker pay rates at the event level.

ActionDefault RoleYour Override
View pay rates on eventsProgram Manager
Modify pay rate per eventAdministrator
Approve pay rate overrideProgram Manager
View worker payment statusFinance Lead
Approve worker paymentFinance Lead
Preferences
Email & SMS Templates
Notification Preferences
TriggerEmailSMSIn-App
Event status change
Worker no-show / 4hr check-in overdue
New worker application
Event pending approval
Payment confirmed
Budget threshold reached (80%)
Worker credential expiring (30 days)
Kit dispatch confirmation
Program report ready
Import completed / failed
Live event escalation (Critical — cannot disable)
Communication Templates
Create reusable message templates for communicating with your internal team and outside partners. System notifications sent to workers (booking confirmations, reminders, payments) are handled automatically by FyldHub.
All
Internal
Partner
Internal
Email
Weekly Program Status Update
To: Program team · Created by Alex M.
Active
Internal
Email
SMS
Budget Threshold Escalation
To: Finance Lead · Created by Sam K.
Active
Partner
Email
Distributor — Program Kick-Off
To: Distributor contacts · Created by Jordan L.
Active
Partner
Email
Retail Account — Event Notice
To: Store manager / buyer · Created by Alex M.
Active
System notifications (worker invites, booking confirmations, payment receipts, compliance reminders) are sent automatically by FyldHub and are not configurable here. Learn more →
Authentication & MFA
Session Management
Data Management
Import data into FyldHub or export records for use in external systems. All imports validate against your account configuration before committing.
Import
Upload data from CSV or Excel. Download a template first to ensure correct formatting. Imports run in the background — you'll receive a notification when complete.
Users
Bulk invite internal team members. Assign roles and program access by column.
Supports: CSV, XLSX · Max 500 rows
Last import: Mar 3, 2026 · 24 users added
Locations
Import store, venue, or market locations with address, chain, tier, and territory assignments.
Supports: CSV, XLSX · Max 5,000 rows
Last import: Feb 20, 2026 · 312 locations added
Budgets
Upload budget allocations by brand, pool, and period. Validates against existing pool structure.
Supports: CSV, XLSX · Max 200 rows
Last import: Jan 15, 2026 · 9 pools configured
Physical Assets
Import your physical asset catalog — item names, brands, specs, and reorder thresholds.
Supports: CSV, XLSX · Max 1,000 rows
Last import: Jan 1, 2026 · 12 assets added
Worker Pre-Approvals
Upload a list of pre-approved worker emails to fast-track their verification for specific programs.
Supports: CSV · Max 2,000 rows
No imports yet
Retail Contacts
Import key account and retail partner contacts — store managers, buyers, district leads.
Supports: CSV, XLSX · Max 1,000 rows
No imports yet
Historical Events
Migrate past event records from a previous agency or system. Used during account setup only.
Supports: CSV · Max 10,000 rows · One-time use
No imports yet
Products / SKU List
Import your brand's product catalog — SKUs, UPCs, product names, and categories.
Supports: CSV, XLSX · Max 2,000 rows
No imports yet
Import History
ObjectDateRowsAddedUpdatedErrorsStatusBy
UsersMar 3, 2026252401CompleteAlex M.
LocationsFeb 20, 202631531203CompleteJordan L.
BudgetsJan 15, 20269900CompleteSam K.
Physical AssetsJan 1, 2026121200CompleteAlex M.
Export
Download records as CSV or Excel. For large datasets, exports run in the background and are emailed when ready. Scheduled exports push to email or external storage on a recurring cadence.
Event Data
All events with status, location, worker, attendance, and performance data.
CSV, XLSX · Filter by date, program, brand
Last export: Mar 10, 2026
Financial / Budget Actuals
Budget pools, spend actuals, variance, and program-level financial breakdown.
CSV, XLSX · Filter by period, brand, pool
Last export: Mar 1, 2026
Worker Performance
Worker ratings, events worked, no-shows, compliance scores, and payment history.
CSV, XLSX · Filter by program, date range
No exports yet
Compliance Reports
Checklist completion rates, incident reports, and compliance scores by event and program.
CSV, PDF · Filter by program, date
Last export: Feb 28, 2026
Program Reports
Program-level summaries with KPIs, spend, events, and consumer engagement metrics.
CSV, XLSX, PDF · Filter by program, period
Last export: Mar 7, 2026
Consumer Engagement
Consumer interaction records, opt-ins, units sampled, and photos — where data capture was enabled.
CSV · Filter by event, program, date
No exports yet
Scheduled Exports
ExportFormatFrequencyDestinationLast RunStatus

Report Auto-Send Templates
Configure reports that build automatically and send on a recurring schedule — weekly, monthly, quarterly, or after every program. Recipients receive a formatted report by email with no manual steps.
Program Weekly Summary
Events completed · Budget utilized · Compliance rate · Top performing workers
All Programs Weekly — Mon 7am PDF + XLSX
To: Alex M., Jordan L., Sam K.
Last sent: Mar 10, 2026
Active
Monthly Budget Actuals
Spend vs. budget by brand · Pool utilization · Variance report · Projected end-of-period
All Brands Monthly — 1st of month XLSX
To: Sam K., CFO distribution list
Last sent: Mar 1, 2026
Active

Events by territory · Sell-through data · Consumer engagements · Compliance scores by distributor
All Distributors Quarterly — End of quarter PDF
To: Marcus R., Tina N., Karen J.
Next send: Apr 1, 2026

Full program recap · All KPIs · Budget final · Worker performance · Recommendations
All Programs Trigger: Program ends PDF
To: Program owner + Finance Lead
Sent 2 times this quarter
Active

Events at their locations · Consumer data · Compliance · Next month schedule preview

To: Buyer/manager contacts per chain

New Report Template
Build a report that generates and sends automatically on a schedule.
Weekly
Monthly
Quarterly
Post-Program
Custom
PDF
XLSX
PDF + XLSX
FyldHub users or external email addresses.
External contacts from your Contacts directory can also be added as recipients.
Custom Fields
Add custom fields to FyldHub objects to capture data specific to your organization. Custom fields appear in the relevant forms, API responses, and exports.
Custom fields are included in all exports and available via the API. They can be mapped to fields in connected systems like Salesforce, SAP, or your BI tool via the Connections tab.
Events
Programs
Locations
Workers
Contacts
Products
Event Custom Fields
Appear on event creation forms and event records. Available to Program Managers and above.
Field Name
API Key
Type
Required
Visible To
Cost Center Code
Maps to ERP cost center for AP sync
cost_center_code
Text
Required
Program Managers+
Consumer Count (Est.)
Estimated attendance entered before event
consumer_count_est
Number
Optional
All users
Retailer Tier
Account importance level for this event
retailer_tier
Dropdown
Required
Program Managers+
Distributor Rep Present
Was a distributor rep on-site?
distributor_rep_present
Yes / No
Optional
All users
Program Custom Fields
Appear on the program creation wizard and program detail view.
Field Name
API Key
Type
Required
Visible To
Campaign ID
Internal campaign code for ERP / Salesforce sync
campaign_id
Text
Required

New Communication Template
Templates can be used to message your internal team or outside partners.
Use {{variable}} syntax for dynamic content.
Create Resource
Resources live in Brand Resources and can be attached to events, programs, or kits.
Kit Contents
List every item that goes in this kit. The logistics team uses this to pack boxes. You can pull items from your Assets list or add custom items.
Item NameQtyFrom Assets Library
Items pulled from Assets will automatically update kit counts when asset inventory changes. Custom items are tracked by name only.
Drop files or click to browse
PDF, PNG, JPG, ZIP — max 50MB
Configure Location Hierarchy
Define the geographic levels that match your organization. You can rename, reorder, add, or remove levels at any time.
Your hierarchy is used throughout FyldHub — for scoping user access, filtering events, and assigning workers. Keep level names simple and consistent across your team.
Current Hierarchy Levels
Drag to reorder. The top level is the broadest (e.g. National). Each level rolls up to the one above it.
1
Top level — broadest geographic scope
↓
2
e.g. Northeast, Southeast, West Coast, Central
↓
3
e.g. Southern California, New England, Mid-Atlantic
↓
4
e.g. Los Angeles, Boston, New York City, Miami
Preview
1 National
2 Region
3 Territory
4 Market
Renaming levels updates all labels across FyldHub. Existing locations are not moved — only their level label changes. Removing a level with assigned locations will require you to reassign those locations.
Add Location
Add a new location to your hierarchy. Select the level first — parent options update automatically. User assignments are managed separately under Users.
2Region
e.g. Northeast
3Territory
e.g. So. California
4Market
e.g. Los Angeles
+Custom
Your own level name
This name will appear in your hierarchy and throughout FyldHub.
Regions roll up to the top-level national entry.
Northeast
Southeast
West Coast
Central
Click to select. A territory can span multiple regions.
Northeast
Southeast
West Coast
Central
New England
Mid-Atlantic
Florida
Carolinas & Georgia
Southern California
Northern California
Pacific Northwest
Click to select. A market rolls up to a territory.
Adding many locations?
Use bulk import to add multiple locations at once from a CSV or Excel file.
Create Program
Programs group events under a named campaign with shared budget, resources, requirements, and reporting.
1 Details
2 Scope & Budget
3 Assets & Resources
4 Worker Requirements

6 Reporting
✓ Review
Program Details
Select which event types are allowed under this program. Only selected categories can be used when creating events. Channel selection above filters the available options.
BA Hub · Brand Promoter
Merchandising Hub · FyldChecker
On-Demand Hub · FyldRunner
Off-Premise categories shown. Change channel above to see other categories.
No selections yet
Assets & Resources
Attach resources to this program. These become defaults for every event created under it — overridable at the event level.
Kit Template
Physical kit dispatched with each event. Inventory tracking in Financial Management.
Brand Digital Assets
Logos, brand guidelines, approved photography. Workers access these in the mobile app.
No selections yet
Event Instructions
Setup procedures, engagement scripts, and close-out checklists for workers.
Recipes
Cocktail recipes workers present to consumers. Multiple can be attached.
No selections yet
Attire Guide
Appearance standards and dress code for workers on this program.
Compliance
Required checklists workers must complete at each event.
Training Requirement
Workers must complete these modules before accepting events under this program.

FyldCard & Payments
Configure how event expenses are funded and how workers are paid under this program.
Event Card (FyldCard)
When enabled, workers receive a loaded virtual card at check-in for event expenses. Requires FyldCard to be active in Financial Settings.
FyldCard enabled for this program
FyldCard disabled
Card Load Settings for this Program
These override the global event-type defaults from FyldCard Settings for events under this program only.
Event Type
Default Load
Max Load
Retail Pop-Up
$
$
Only event categories selected in Step 1 are shown. Adjust global defaults in Financial → FyldCard.

How workers are paid for events under this program. Overrides the global financial setting.
Optionally override the global rate card for this program. Useful for premium campaigns with elevated pay.

Who receives program performance reports. The program owner always receives reports.

Cocktail recipes workers present to consumers. Displayed in the worker mobile app during the event.

Create Training Module
Workers must complete this before accepting events under this program. Completion is tracked automatically.

Active Add-Ons
Add-ons layer intelligence and capabilities on top of your hub execution data. All usage-based add-on fees appear in the Usage tab.
FyldCard Active
Virtual event expense cards for workers. Loaded at check-in, reclaimed at checkout.
$399 / mo
+ 5% txn + $0.50 flat per txn
Benchmarking Active
Compare execution performance across brands, markets, and programs. Competitive shelf benchmarks.
$3,000 / mo
+ $1.50 per tracked store
Predictive Performance Active
AI-driven forecasts for event outcomes, worker performance, and market ROI. Identifies risk before execution.
$5,000 / mo
+ $0.75 per tracked store
POS / Sales Correlation Active
Correlates field execution events with point-of-sale data to measure direct sales lift per activation.
Variable
$2.50 per POS match
Available Add-Ons (not active)
Geospatial Intelligence $0.30/event + $0.003/ping
Survey Insights (Full) Variable per demo event
Data Marketplace $10,000/mo + $8/tracked store
Custom ML Pipelines $2,083/mo
Retailer Deep Dive $10,000/mo
Payment
Managed securely via Stripe

Partner Information
🏢
Agency
Executes work on your behalf
🚚
Distributor
Territory distribution partner
🏪
Retailer
Account / store chain
➕
Other
Broker, 3PL, consultant…
Brand Activations Agency
Experiential, events, tastings, demos, pop-ups, sponsorships. Works with Brand Promoters.
Merchandising Agency
Shelf audits, planogram compliance, display installations, retail execution. Works with FyldCheckers.
Staffing Agency
Provides workers only. Does not manage programs or events. No execution responsibility.
Logistics / 3PL Agency
Handles kit storage, shipping, POS delivery, and on-demand logistics. Works with FyldRunners.
Select all that apply — this determines the permissions we recommend on the next step.
Select all that apply — this determines the permissions we recommend on the next step.
Distributors get territory-level visibility into your activation calendar and can submit field requests. They do not see financials, internal users, or other partners. A full Distributor tenant upgrade path is available in Phase 2.
Retailers get read-only access to activation schedules and geo-stamped compliance documentation for their own store locations only. They cannot see other retailers, financials, or internal users.
Access Scope & Permissions
Partners only see data within their assigned scope. They cannot view other agencies, your internal financials, or data outside these boundaries.
Recommended permissions based on your selections
National
Northeast
Southeast
Midwest
West Coast
Southwest
Off-Premise
On-Premise
Special Events
Configure exactly what this partner can do inside your account.
Delegated Execution: This partner will actively execute work inside your account. Every record they create — events, check-ins, photos, submissions — is owned by your account, not theirs. They cannot see each other or your internal team's data.
Financial Terms & Worker Pay
Define agency compensation and who is responsible for paying workers. All worker payments — regardless of responsible party — are processed through FyldHub's payment infrastructure.
Event Execution Rate
$ /hr
$
Agency Management Fee
$ per event
% of total event cost
Retainer
A retainer is a pre-agreed budget ceiling. Hourly rates and event fees draw against it each period. You are notified when the balance reaches 20%.
$
Worker Pay Responsibility
All worker payments must flow through FyldHub's payment infrastructure — no exceptions. This ensures workers are paid on time and have access to instant pay regardless of who funds the payment.
Stripe Connect & Treasury required
When you add this partner, FyldHub will send them an email invitation to complete Stripe Connect onboarding. They must connect a Stripe account and set up a FyldHub Treasury wallet before they can fund worker payments. Access is provisionally granted while onboarding is pending, but worker pay is suspended until setup is complete.
Worker Pay Schedule
Applies regardless of who funds payments. Workers are notified of their pay timeline in the app after each event is approved.
Standard Pay Included
Workers are paid within 3 business days of event approval. No fee to the worker. Funds are disbursed automatically — no manual action required.
Instant Pay Worker-elected
Workers can elect to receive pay immediately after approval for a small processing fee (typically 1–2%, charged to the worker). Available in the worker app at all times.
FyldHub guarantees worker payment on the standard schedule regardless of agency or supplier cash flow. If the responsible party fails to fund on time, FyldHub reserves the right to pause the partner's access and recover funds.
No selections yet
Create Budget
Define a new budget for a brand. You'll add pools and assign scope after creating the top-level budget.
1 Brand & Type
2 Pools
3 Review
Brand Activation — field execution events, demos, sponsorships.
$
Total budget envelope. Pools will allocate from this amount.
Define one or more budget pools. Each pool allocates a portion of the total budget to a specific channel, territory, or program. You can add more pools after saving.
Total budget: —  |  Allocated in pools below: $0  |  Unallocated: —
Review before saving

Budget will be created immediately. You can add sub-pools, link programs, and adjust amounts from the Budget Control Center at any time.

Internal Users
Add New Role
Name your role, configure module-level access, then set sub-action permissions.
1 Name & Base
2 Set Permissions
Choose the closest predefined role as your starting point. This pre-fills the module access matrix — you adjust from there.
Program Owner
Creates programs, approves launches
Program Manager
Manages active programs and events
Field Team
Field execution and requests
Workforce Coordinator
Worker invites and applications
Finance Manager
Budget pools and approvals
Ops Manager
Assets and logistics management
Analyst / Viewer
Full read-only access
Blank Role
Start from scratch — No Access on all modules
Click any module to expand and set permissions per feature (View / Create / Edit / Delete / Approve). Changes from the base role are highlighted. All modules start with the base role defaults.
Edit Permissions
Adjust module access and sub-action controls
Module Access
Sub-Actions Phase 2
Role Info
Click any module to expand and configure feature-level permissions.
Phase 2 / Enterprise Feature
Sub-action controls allow View / Create / Edit / Delete / Approve to be set per individual feature within each module. Overrides module-level access for specific actions.
Programs & Events
Feature
View
Create
Edit
Delete
Approve
View program list

Submit activation request

View

Role Details
Type—
Users assigned—
Last modified—
System roles cannot be renamed or deleted. You can copy this role as a base for a custom role.
Edit User
Platform Access
Hub Access
Account Status
Changes to role or scope take effect immediately on next login. The user will receive an email notification.
Invite Team Member
They'll receive an email to set up their account. They complete their profile on first login.
Only hubs licensed by your account are shown. Users can only access hubs assigned to them.
Select the geographic level this user is responsible for.
Access to all regions, territories, and markets — no restriction.
Invitation expires in 7 days. Location scope can be edited at any time from the Users table.
Workflows
Automate event lifecycle transitions, approval routing, and notifications. Rules fire based on status changes, schedule triggers, or field conditions.
Auto-Advance: Post-Event → Pending Approval
When recap is submitted with all required photos, automatically advance event status.
Trigger: Recap submitted · Applies to: All hubs · Delay: Immediate
Active
SLA Alert: Approval Overdue 24h
Notify Program Manager and Org Admin when a recap has been in Pending Approval for more than 24 hours.
Trigger: Timer · Threshold: 24 hours · Recipients: Program Manager, Org Admin
Active
Kit Dispatch: Auto-assign FyldRunner on Confirmation
When an On-Demand event reaches Confirmed, auto-create a kit dispatch task and notify the assigned FyldRunner.
Trigger: Status → Confirmed · Hub: On-Demand · Status: Draft
Draft
Visual Workflow Builder — Coming Next Release
Build custom automation with a visual condition editor. Multi-step approvals, conditional branching, time-based triggers, and cross-hub logic in active development.
Audit Log
Complete record of platform actions including user changes, event lifecycle transitions, permission modifications, and system events. Retained for 12 months.
